title: 'StravaLeaks': How Le Monde located 18,000 French military personnel with a fitness app
subhead: For more than two years, Le Monde has been tracking a significant proportion of France's military personnel on Strava, a popular fitness app, on almost a daily basis. Their movements can be monitored, including in sensitive areas, putting both military operations and the servicemembers involved in them at risk.

title: Israel braces for delayed economic fallout of war in Iran
Emmanuel Grégoire takes office as the new Paris mayor
Flavel, a victim of police violence, tells his story: 'If there hadn't been videos, no one would ever have believed me'
subhead: On March 16, the former musician went out to order food in Noisiel, in the eastern Paris suburbs. Police violently arrested him, as seen in a video widely shared online. He described to Le Monde the events that led him to file a complaint with the internal affairs agency.

title: Adrien Bilal, winner of the 2026 Best Young Economist Award: 'Climate change will cost 50% of GDP by 2100'
subhead: The winner of the 27th edition of the prize, created in 2000 by Le Monde and Le Cercle des Economistes, has developed a model that better captures the global impacts of climate disruption.

title: Is it ever a good idea to... wear leather pants?
subhead: In fashion, as in life, it is important to know the lines you should not cross. Or do so deliberately.

title: In wartime Tehran, rare photos capture daily life under bombardment
subhead: The photographs taken by an Iranian photographer and published by Le Monde offer a unique window into the capital's daily life. With 14 million inhabitants, Tehran has been shrouded in a media blackout since February 28. For his safety, the photographer has requested anonymity.

Christian Jambet, Islamic scholar: 'The true leaders of Iran are now soldiers who want to spread the Islamic Revolution beyond Iran's border'
subhead: The philosopher and member of the Académie Française, an expert on Shiite spiritualities, shares his thoughts on the ongoing war and the 'degeneration' of the Iranian regime.

title: Revealed: Satellite image shows mysterious cargo at Iranian nuclear site in 2025
subhead: Le Monde reveals an image of a convoy at the gates of a major underground nuclear complex in Iran. According to several experts, it could be part of the country's stockpile of enriched uranium.

title: 'No Kings' protests against Trump focus on war in Iran: 'The US is on the wrong side of history'
subhead: Millions turned out to protests across the US against the Trump administration, with the movement's epicenter located in the twin cities of Minneapolis-Saint Paul, after two Americans were killed by federal agents there in January.
